codeReview.summary: Implementation is correct, well-documented, and matches the feature specification. ipi_kick_idle_core() is called under STATE lock at all three call sites (try_wake_impl, spawn_user, start_suspended_threads). send_ipi uses raw MSR without nomem (correct for system register side-effects). SGI 0 dispatch in irq_handler is cleanly separated from timer and device IRQ paths — no TICKS increment, no timer::handle_irq, no interrupt::handle_irq(0). schedule() and end_of_interrupt() correctly run for all IRQ types including SGI 0. WFE→WFI change is correct (WFI wakes on any interrupt including IPI; WFE requires SEV which GICv3 IPIs don't generate). LOCK-ORDERING.md section 6 thoroughly documents the sender/handler paths, no-deadlock argument, and is_idle race prevention. SAFETY comments on all unsafe blocks are accurate. 13 new tests cover all specified validation assertions.

issues.description: ipi_kick_idle_core sends to only the FIRST idle core found. In start_suspended_threads, multiple threads can be moved to the ready queue in one call, but only one idle core is woken. Under tickless idle (no periodic timer), the remaining threads could be stranded until the single woken core finishes its work and triggers another scheduling event, or until an unrelated interrupt wakes other cores. This is per-spec ('same logic as try_wake') but is a latency concern for multi-thread process starts under tickless. Consider a future enhancement to IPI N idle cores when N threads are enqueued.

SGI 0 dispatch tests use a model function (irq_dispatch_model) that mirrors the irq_handler logic rather than testing the actual kernel code. This is the established pattern (kernel code is bare-metal, not directly unit-testable), but means a dispatch logic change in main.rs requires a parallel update to the test model. The const SGI_IPI is defined independently in both main.rs and the test file — if the value ever diverged, the test wouldn't catch it. Consider importing from a shared location, though for a hardware constant (SGI 0 = 0) this is low risk.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents IPI-scheduler lock interaction conventions and tickless WFI requirement. No gaps found — the worker's implementation matches all documented conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md §Coding Conventions: 'IPI send (raw msr) is safe inside scheduler lock' and §Tickless specifics: 'spawn_user and start_suspended_threads must IPI idle cores' / 'Idle loops must use WFI (not WFE)' — all implemented as specified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the os-worker skill procedure correctly. Handoff confirms followedProcedure: true with no deviations. Test-first approach verified (13 new tests), full suite run, build, stress/integration/smoke all passed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification shows 7 commands run: test suite (1751 pass), build (clean), stress (48s pass), integration (18 checks OK), smoke (pass), grep wfe (zero matches), grep wfi (2 matches).

summary: PASS. Clean implementation of IPI-driven wakeup across 5 files (385 insertions, 11 deletions). Core logic in ipi_kick_idle_core() is simple, correct, and well-positioned at all three call sites under STATE lock. Unsafe code is properly annotated — send_ipi omits nomem (correct for MSR side-effects), WFI uses nomem (correct for hint instruction). LOCK-ORDERING.md addition is thorough and accurately reasons about the no-deadlock and no-race properties. 13 new tests cover all validation assertions (VAL-IPI-002 through VAL-IPI-012). One suggestion: consider IPIing multiple idle cores when start_suspended_threads enqueues multiple threads, to avoid tickless latency. One non-blocking note: model-based IRQ dispatch tests duplicate constants and logic from main.rs.
